Name: Alice Bentinck
Description:
Alice Bentinck, co-founder and General Partner at Entrepreneur First (EF), has played a significant role in the growth of the world's leading talent investor. Since 2011, EF has invested time and money in over 1,200 individuals, resulting in the creation of 200+ technology companies with a combined valuation of over $1.5 billion.

The company expanded its programs to six global locations and recently closed a $115 million global fund for further investments.

Apart from her work at EF, Alice Bentinck is the co-founder of Code First: Girls, a not-for-profit organisation dedicated to teaching women to code for free. Under her leadership, the organisation surpassed its goal of teaching 20,000 women to code by reaching over 10,000 women earlier this year.

Alice Bentinck, the Angel Investor

As an angel investor, Alice Bentinck invested in Supercritical and koru Kids

Supercritical (greentech)

Supercritical serves as a carbon removal platform designed to expedite your climate initiatives.

Climate leaders leverage Supercritical to discover, comprehend, and acquire a collection of influential carbon removal credits, thereby achieving their sustainability objectives with assurance and efficiency. Alice invested in Supercritical with fellow angel investor Amit Gudka

Koru Kids

Koru Kids is a UK-based startup that focuses on providing childcare services. Founded by Rachel Carrell, the company is known for its platform that connects families with qualified and experienced after-school nannies.

Koru Kids aims to make high-quality child care more accessible and convenient for working parents by offering personalised and reliable services.

Alice Bentinck and Entrepreneur First

Alice Bentinck's career began at McKinsey & Co, where she met Entrepreneur First co-founder, Matt Clifford.

Entrepreneur First has become a significant player in the startup ecosystem, with over 500 startups created and a combined portfolio valued at over $6 billion as of 2022. The company's international presence expanded to Singapore in 2016, and in 2017, it secured a $12.4 million investment led by Reid Hoffman, co-founder of LinkedIn and Partner at Greylock.

Alice Bentinck's commitment to addressing gender disparities in the tech industry led her to co-found Code First: Girls in 2012. This non-profit has become the largest provider of free coding courses for women in the UK, having taught over 25,000 women to code.

Alice Bentinck recognition and awards

In recognition of her contributions to business, she was awarded an MBE in the 2016 Queen's Birthday Honours. Besides her entrepreneurial endeavours, Alice is a member of the UK AI Council, representing the country's AI sector internationally.

In February 2022, Alice was appointed to Prime Minister Boris Johnson's Business Council, emphasising her influence and leadership in the business community.

In December 2023, it was announced that Alice Bentinck would take over as CEO of Entrepreneur First, succeeding Matt Clifford, who stepped down to focus on opportunities in artificial intelligence.

Name: Amit Gudka
Description:
Amit Gudka is an angel investor and the founder/CEO of Field, a battery energy storage company.

Amit Gudka, the angel investor

As an angel investor, Amit Gudka invested in the energy and greentech startup Supercritical with Alice Bentinck.

Supercritical is a carbon removal marketplace that focuses primarily on serving tech companies aiming to achieve "net zero" carbon emissions.

Supercritical operates by aggregating business demand for advanced carbon removal technologies, providing companies with access to innovative solutions that extend beyond traditional methods like tree planting.

The marketplace enables companies to purchase "fully-vetted and qualified" carbon removal credits in accordance with the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) industry standard.

Amit Gudka and Field Energy

Field Energy has successfully secured Â£100 million in new funding from DIF Capital Partners, a Dutch infrastructure investor.

The funds are earmarked for the company's European expansion plans and to accelerate the rollout of renewable energy infrastructure.

Field Energy, which already operates several battery storage sites in the UK, is expected to officially announce the fundraising in the coming weeks.

This marks Field's first funding round since last year, following a Â£30 million equity raise and a Â£47 million debt facility. The investment reflects the growing significance of battery storage companies, like Field, in contributing to global decarbonization efforts and achieving net-zero targets.

Amitâ€™s Background

Prior to launching Field, he co-founded Bulb, a tech-led renewable energy supplier, in 2015, along with Co-Founder Hayden Wood.

Bulb was the fastest-growing energy supplier in the UK, supplying over 1.7 million households in the UK and abroad. Before venturing into the renewable energy sector, Amit Gudka worked as an energy trader at Barclays for eight years, focusing on European gas and electricity markets.

He holds a mathematics degree from the University of Cambridge and is also involved in running an independent record label in his spare time.

Name: Hermann Hauser
Description:
Dr. Hermann Hauser is a renowned serial entrepreneur, Cambridge angel investor and co-founder of Amadeus Capital Partners. He has played a pivotal role in shaping Cambridge's innovative tech landscape.

Dr Hermann Hauser, the angel investor

Dr Hermann Hauser is a prolific angel investor in startups such as: Cogna, NXRT, Usound, Oxford Ionix, byrd, QuantrolOx, Easelink, Hyperganic.

Angel Investment: Cogna

Hermann invested in Cogna as an angel investor with Stan Boland.

Cogna is a company specialising in artificial intelligence (AI) platforms tailored for custom software development for traditional industries such as manufacturing and civil engineering. Their platform is designed to simplify intricate resource allocation processes, optimising utilisation and enhancing efficiency.

Angel Investment: byrd

Byrd designed to provide scalable logistics solutions for online shops, empowering them to reach their full potential. Through an intuitive application, the platform seamlessly integrates with e-commerce shops, offering real-time stock tracking and a convenient first-mile shipping service.

This service includes item pick-up and packaging, all accessible with a simple click on a mobile application. In essence, the platform aims to streamline the shipping process for both private and business customers, offering a one-click solution for efficient and hassle-free item shipments.

Dr Hermann Hauser and Amadeus Capital Partners

Hermann Hauser founded the venture capital firm Amadeus Capital Partners with Anne Glover in 1997.

Amadeus is a prominent technology venture capital firm with a team of over 40 professionals. Specialising in early-stage and growth capital investments, Amadeus seeks to support companies with the potential to become global leaders.

The firm has a diverse investment portfolio, spanning various sectors, including AI and machine learning, consumer services, cybersecurity, digital health, medical technology, digital media, enterprise software, fintech, insurtech, regtech, semiconductors, displays, and telecoms infrastructure.

Amadeus Capital Partners has a presence in London, Cambridge, and Oxford in the UK, as well as Lausanne and San Francisco.

Hermann Hauserâ€™s background

Born in Vienna, he initially came to Cambridge at 15 to learn English and later returned for his Ph.D. in Physics at the Cavendish Laboratory.

With a history of founding and co-founding over 20 technology companies, including Acorn Computers and the development of the BBC Microcomputer, Dr. Hauser has left a lasting impact on the technology industry. His ventures span various sectors such as Active Book Company, Virata, Net Products, and Cambridge Network Limited.

Notable accolades include being named the UK's "Computer Personality of the Year" in 1984 and receiving the Mountbatten Medal for outstanding contributions to information technology in 2000. He was honoured with an honorary CBE in 2001 and a knighthood (KBE) in 2015 for his services to engineering and industry.

Dr. Hauser's involvement extends to advisory roles, serving on boards, and contributing to technology and innovation initiatives. His contributions to stem cell research and leadership in the East Anglia Stem Cell research network underscore his commitment to advancing scientific endeavours.

As a Fellow of the Institute of Physics and the Royal Academy of Engineering, and an Honorary Fellow of King's College, Cambridge, Dr. Hermann Hauser continues to be a driving force in the intersection of technology, innovation, and entrepreneurship.

Name: Andrew Robb
Description:
Andrew Robb, a prominent figure in the world of angel investing, has left an indelible mark on the startup landscape with his strategic investments in innovative ventures.

Boasting a profound connection to marketplaces, Robb has strategically supported and invested in a range of startups, including GetHarley, Super Payments, and others

This article delves into his notable contributions, exploring his involvement in shaping the trajectory of startups and his pivotal role in the success of companies like Farfetch.

With a background spanning strategy consulting, digital music startups, and leadership in e-commerce, Robb's diverse expertise and passion for technology-led businesses make him a key player in the entrepreneurial ecosystem.

Andrew Robb, the angel investor

Andrew Robb is a very active angel investor. With a deep connection to marketplaces, he has invested in the following startups: GetHarley, Patch, Colette, Localyze, Avant Arte, Bridebook, Gaia, Hubble, Otta, Plain, Sequence, Sprout, Super Payments, Sylvera, The Garden

Angel investment: GetHarley

Founded in 2019 by Charmaine Chow, GetHarley is a platform that connects consumers with skin care clinicians, including dermatologists and plastic surgeons.

Users can access qualified professionals who provide recommendations for skincare products and treatments for various conditions such as acne, uneven skin tone, hyperpigmentation, rosacea, and melasma.

GetHarley has a strong angel investor base including Andrew Robb but also Sian Keane, John Veichmanis, Vincenzo Iozzo, James Vincent.

Angel investment: Super Payments

Launched in 2021 by Samir Desai, renowned for his role at Funding Circle, SuperPayments has built a marketplace centred around payment processing.

The platform connects brands, offering them a fresh distribution channel to reach consumers. A distinctive feature of SuperPayments is its provision of cashback to consumers upon making purchases through the platform.

Andrew Robb was an angel investor in Super Payments along with Sir Ron Kalifa OBE, Edward Wray, Peter Jackson, Gokul Rajaram, and Maria Raga.

Andrew and Farfetch

Andrew played a pivotal role in leading Farfetch as Chief Operating Officer and Executive Board member for over a decade, guiding the company from Series A to a successful $6 billion IPO in New York.

In this role, he oversaw marketing, operations, international, and VIP teams, contributing to Farfetch's emergence as a global leader in luxury fashion e-commerce.

With over 20 years of experience in e-commerce, Andrew began his career with Gemini Consulting and later served as Business Development Director at Peoplesound.com, a digital music startup. He also worked in the category development team of eBay Australia and served as the Managing Director of Cocosa.com.

In addition to his achievements at Farfetch, Andrew has founded a fashion e-commerce startup and played a key role in driving category development for eBay in Australia during its hyper-growth phase.

Andrewâ€™s background

Andrew's educational background includes a B.A. from the University of Oxford and an M.B.A. from INSEAD. As a senior leader in e-commerce, his strengths encompass leadership, general management, strategy, marketing, product development, business development, strategic partnerships, and venture financing.

He shows a passion for creating and growing technology-led businesses through building exceptional teams.

Overall, Andrew Robb brings a diverse mix of experience gained from strategy consulting, a digital music startup, a leading e-commerce company, and the UK's largest magazine publisher. His overarching goal is to create new businesses, products, and services that add value to people and businesses.

Name: Helen Oldham
Description:
Helen Oldham is a prominent figure in the Northern tech startup scene, serving as a Founding Board Director of NorthInvest, a not-for-profit organisation connecting early-stage investors with Northern-based tech startups.

A very active startup investor

In 2020, she established Fund Her North, a collective of 28 women in venture capital and angel groups aimed at supporting female founders in gaining equal access to finance.

Additionally, Helen is involved in various roles, including being a Non-Executive Director (NED), advisor, mentor, angel investor, and Chair of the Trustees for Smart Works Leeds, a charity supporting unemployed women.

Her career spans leadership roles in notable newspapers like The Yorkshire Post and The Scotsman. Helen's passion lies in supporting female entrepreneurs, leaders, and Northern SMEs to achieve their potential and implement ambitious growth strategies.

Helen is also the Co-Founder of Lifted Ventures with the angel investor Jordan Dargue, an early-stage investment connector focused on making regional funding accessible to all, regardless of gender, ethnicity, or diversity.

She is actively involved in the UK Business Angels Association (UKBAA) and the Women Angel Investment taskforce.

Helenâ€™s thoughts on the Leeds startup ecosystem.

Discussing the economic outlook for Leeds and the wider region, Helen draws attention to the existing collaboration, particularly in innovation and digitization. Despite economic challenges, she sees positivity in these sectors, fostering the growth of early-stage businesses.

Helen believes that Leeds has a wealth of innovative startups, especially in medtech, biotech, green energy, and clean tech. To accelerate growth, she highlights the need to promote the success of these startups and attract more businesses to scale in the region.

The Leeds Innovation Arc, a strategic plan for inclusive and economic growth, is expected to generate greater investor interest nationally and globally.

In the interview, Helen elaborates on NorthInvest's role in driving regional growth. As a fast-growing Angel investor network, NorthInvest actively onboards new investors, including those previously unfamiliar with angel investing.

Helen emphasises the organisation's focus on inclusive growth, running workshops and events to encourage women to become Angel investors. She mentions the success of Fund Her North, supporting female founders and investors.

For startup businesses seeking funding opportunities, Helen advises strategic planning, defining propositions, clear business plans, and establishing strong connections with funding partnerships.

She highlights the importance of communities like Nexus, collaborating with them on projects to support business founders in becoming investment-ready and accessing advice and expertise.

Overall, Helen's work showcases a commitment to fostering innovation, inclusivity, and economic growth in the Northern tech startup ecosystem.

Name: Stan Boland
Description:
Stan Boland, a seasoned entrepreneur, angel investor and ex-CEO of FiveAI, has had a diverse and successful career in the technology and semiconductor industry.

Stan Boland, the angel

Stan Boland has invested as an angel investor in TickX and Cogna.

Angel Investment: Cogna

Stan Boland invested in Cogna along with the following angels: Hermann Hauser, Professor Neil Lawrence, Zayd Enam, Tim Shi, Ping Wu, Aviv Nevo, and Nick Elliot.

At the core of Cogna's innovative approach is an AI-driven Software as a Service (SaaS) platform that streamlines data integration across various business functions.

This empowers domain experts to collaborate with AI in creating bespoke software tools, facilitating analysis, optimizations, and decisions that result in substantial operational improvements.

Cogna's focus on addressing the inherent complexity of diverse processes in sectors like civil engineering and manufacturing sets it apart, as off-the-shelf solutions often fall short in these domains.

Early success and positive reception from clients, including Cadent Gas, underscore the potential transformative impact of Cogna's technology on real-world operations and efficiency.

Angel Investment: TickX

TickX is a platform founded in 2015 by childhood friends Steve Pearce and Sam Coley. Originally conceived as a one-stop shop for fans to discover and purchase event tickets, TickX has evolved to offer technology that benefits producers and live entertainment organisers.

In 2020, the company shifted its mission to unlock the power of direct bookings for the events industry.

TickX operates as a transformational layer situated between marketing teams and platforms, and the ticketing systems it integrates with. Instead of replicating the entire feature set of ticketing systems, TickX focuses on providing a valuable layer on top, described as the 'tip of the iceberg.' This approach aims to enhance ticket sales and marketing efficiencies while allowing ticketing partners to continue functioning as the operational engine for successful ticketing operations.

The platform has been involved in powering the ticketing and marketing efforts of major names in the global entertainment industry since 2020.

Stan Boland and FiveAI

His most recent venture, FiveAI, founded in 2015, focused on developing automated driving software. Initially demonstrating a highly automated online driving system for urban driving, FiveAI pivoted to create an integrated, cloud-based development ecosystem for testing automated driving systems in low-fidelity simulation.

Bosch acquired FiveAI in 2022, marking Boland's third major exit. Prior to this, he successfully co-founded and led Element 14 Inc. and Icera Semiconductor Inc., both acquired by Broadcom and Nvidia, with a combined sale price exceeding $1.1 billion.

Stanâ€™s background

Graduating in Physics from the University of Cambridge, Boland initially worked in roles at Rolls-Royce Aerospace, Bell Group, and Bricom. His career took a significant turn in the 1980s when he worked for Robert Holmes Ã Court, and later at ICL from 1990 to 1997, where he delved into the technology sector.

In 1997, Boland joined Acorn Computers, which eventually spun out as Element 14. Broadcom acquired Element 14 in 2000 for $640 million, leading to Boland becoming the Vice President of its DSL business unit.

His directorship extended to ARM Holdings as well. In 2002, Boland co-founded Icera, a venture that was later acquired by Nvidia in 2011 for $367 million plus an undisclosed staff retention amount.

Boland's entrepreneurial journey continued as he assumed the role of CEO at Neul in April 2013. Additionally, since January 2014, he has been the Chairman of NMI, the UK national association for companies in the electronics systems sector.

Known for his significant contributions to the semiconductor industry, Boland's achievements include being a Board member of ARM Holdings and serving as CEO of Acorn Group plc. His educational background includes a degree in Physics and Theoretical Physics from the University of Cambridge, and he is a member of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers, as well as a fellow of the Institute of Chartered Management Accountants.

Boland's current focus as the CEO of FiveAI reflects his commitment to building robust autonomous driving software.

Name: Andy Phillipps
Description:
Andy Phillipps, a London-based entrepreneur, angel investor, and Venture Partner, has played key roles in various successful ventures.

He currently serves as the Chairman/Director for YPlan, Chairman for Reevoo, IQ Capital Partners, and greentraveller.co.uk. Previously, he co-founded Active Hotels and served as CEO, and was Chairman for toptable.co.uk.

Phillipps invests in clean technology, consumer internet, enterprise software, and mobile-based companies in London.

Andy Philipps, the angel investor

As an angel investor, Andy Philipps has invested in Nous, ThirdEye Labs, Wevat, We Got POP, Wholi, Encore, MyNextRun, Signal AI, YPlan, Zoopla, Reevoo, Perkbox

Angel Investment: Zoopla

Zoopla is an online platform, founded in 2007, that serves as a property website and app. It offers users access to a diverse range of over a million properties and provides tools for efficient filtering.

Zoopla also supplies reliable house price estimates to assist users in making informed decisions. While its primary focus is on practical aspects such as price, location, and features, the platform acknowledges the emotional significance of finding a home and aims to help users discover properties that align with their personal feelings and experiences.

Angel Investment: Signal AI

Signal AI distinguishes itself by combining discriminative and generative AI techniques to provide actionable and relevant insights. Discriminative AI focuses on making specific predictions or classifications based on given data, while generative AI involves creating new data samples.

Signal AI's solutions aim to empower influential corporations worldwide, with over 650 customers, by offering them the tools to make informed decisions with confidence.

Andyâ€™s background

Born in 1968 in Woking, Surrey, Phillipps attended Allen House preparatory school and later Bradfield College. Describing himself as a generalist with interests in English, sciences, and maths, he emphasises the importance of exposure to both disciplines.

In 2010, Phillipps transitioned to angel investing, actively supporting 70 startups with a combined market cap exceeding $10 billion and over 5000 employees.

He believes his value lies in providing entrepreneurs with smarts, experience, and a candid sounding board for challenging decisions.

Phillipps acknowledges the industry's standard success rate, emphasising the power law nature of angel investing. Despite the challenges, he remains committed to investing in people whose values align with his own, aiming to contribute positively to the world.

Andy Philipps and Active Hotels

As a co-founder of Active Hotels, Phillipps witnessed its growth into the largest online booking company in Europe. His investment philosophy revolves around supporting intellectually smart individuals with the ability to motivate and recruit teams, make quick decisions, and listen to diverse opinions.

Name: Greg Marsh
Description:
Greg Marsh, a prominent figure in entrepreneurship and venture capital, has made diverse contributions across investment, academia, and social impact.

As an angel investor, Marsh strategically backs ventures like Deliveroo and Tide, reshaping conventional paradigms. His entrepreneurial ventures, notably onefinestay, exemplify his market acumen.

Marsh's latest endeavour, Nous, tackles the UK's cost-of-living crisis. Additionally, his role as an educator at institutions like Harvard Business School underscores his commitment to shaping future business leaders. In essence, Greg Marsh epitomises the essence of a visionary entrepreneur, investor, and thought leader.

Greg Marsh, the angel investor

As an angel investor, Greg Marsh has invested in Deliveroo, flatfair, Hokodo, Onfido, Weezy, Communion, Snap It Limited, Flown, Holy, Coniq, Switched, Weaver.build, Klevio, Tide

Angel Investment: Tide

Tide, founded in 2015 by George Bevis, emerged onto the financial scene with a clear mission: to simplify business banking for SMEs. What began as a startup has swiftly evolved into a thriving ecosystem, boasting a community of over 750,000 small businesses united by their dedication to their craft. While the United Kingdom served as Tide's initial stomping ground, the company's expansion has seen it extend its reach to India since 2022.

Angel Investment: Deliveroo

In 2013, when Will Shu relocated to London, he was struck by the plethora of fantastic restaurants in the city, yet dismayed by the limited options for food delivery. Determined to bridge this gap, Shu embarked on a personal crusade to revolutionise the dining experience by bringing the finest local eateries directly to people's doorsteps.

Enter Deliveroo, the brainchild of Shu, which has since evolved into a hyperlocal three-sided marketplace, seamlessly connecting local consumers, restaurants, and grocers, facilitated by a fleet of dedicated riders. The company's mission is clear: to fulfil the urgent and emotional need for food in under 30 minutes.

Greg Marsh and onefinestay

After establishing himself as a venture capital investor with Index Ventures in London, Marsh ventured into entrepreneurship by founding onefinestay in 2009.

This innovative luxury accommodation marketplace, inspired by a trip to Pisa, offered travellers the experience of hotel services in upscale homes while the owners were away.

Under Marsh's leadership, onefinestay flourished, expanding its operations internationally and attracting a considerable team. In 2016, the company was acquired by AccorHotels in a deal exceeding $250 million, marking a significant milestone in Marsh's entrepreneurial journey. Following the acquisition, Marsh transitioned from his roles as CEO and Chairman, leaving behind a legacy of success.

Greg Marsh and Nous

Currently, Marsh continues his entrepreneurial endeavours with Nous, a venture aimed at assisting UK households amidst the cost-of-living crisis. With his diverse background and extensive experience, Marsh remains a notable figure in both the business and social spheres, embodying the qualities of a visionary entrepreneur and advocate for positive change.

Greg Marsh and Academia

Marsh's expertise extends beyond entrepreneurship; he has also made notable contributions to academia. He has taught at esteemed institutions such as Harvard Business School and Imperial College Business School, sharing his insights on venture capital and entrepreneurship with future business leaders.

Name: Jordan Dargue
Description:
In the world of startup ecosystems and innovative enterprises, few individuals stand out as true champions of growth and investment.

Jordan Dargue, a founding director, non-executive director, and Women Angel of the North, is one such figure who has carved a niche for herself in the investment landscape.

With a passion for fostering innovation and a proven track record, Jordan plays a pivotal role in supporting startups and scaling organisations to reach their full potential.

Angel Investor: Investment Focus

Jordan's investment portfolio is distinctive for its geographic concentration in the North of England, reflecting her commitment to the local business community.

Her focus on Seed Enterprise Investment Scheme (SEIS) investments, particularly in tech sectors such as Biotech, Advanced Manufacturing, SaaS, and media tech, showcases a strategic approach to nurturing industries that are driving the future.

Noteworthy is her investment in a unique dog community app, k9Nation, showcasing her interest in diverse and innovative ventures.

As an angel investor, Jordan has invested in 8 companies and completed 11 rounds of investment, showcasing both the breadth and depth of her engagement in the startup ecosystem.

Championing Women in Business

Beyond her impressive investment track record, Jordan Dargue is making waves as a champion for diversity in entrepreneurship.

Having invested in 3 female-founded or co-founded businesses, she has pledged to focus exclusively on supporting women-founded businesses going forward.

In 2021, she co-founded Women Angels of the North angel syndicate under the Fund Her North initiative, an endeavour that has rapidly gained traction with 37 women now onboarded and 8 investments made into female founders.

In 2024, Jordan joined forces with angel investor Helen Oldham and launched an angel investment network to back women-led start-ups in the north of England called Lifted Ventures.

Leadership and Expertise

Jordan's leadership extends beyond her investment activities.

As a mentor and advisory board member, she guides startups and high-growth businesses across the UK, connecting them with the right investors and providing support in commercialization, marketing, recruitment, and retention strategies.

Her transformative influence is evident in orchestrating multi-million equity-based investment rounds and cultivating award-winning investor networks.

Multi-Sector Impact

Jordan's impact spans a multitude of sectors, including Construction, Housing Development, Technology, Investment, Property, Retail, and Recruitment.

Her ability to identify opportunities for innovation and optimum growth has been a driving force in helping businesses establish, launch, and scale.

In collaboration with operational and revenue-generating organisations, Jordan enhances operational efficiency, leading to rapid increases in EBITDA.

Jordan Dargue's journey as a senior business leader, investor, and growth consultant is an inspiring testament to the transformative power of strategic investments and unwavering support for innovation.

He is known for his ability to create long-term business strategies, manage diverse personalities on a board, and his strong advocacy for mentoring executive management to accomplish short and long-term business objectives. Currently, he holds several prominent positions including Chairman of Avon-Rubber PLC, PE Chairman of Surrey Nano Systems, and Non-executive Director of Power Photonics. He also serves as a board member of ADS, the premier trade organisation for companies in the UK Aerospace, Defence, Security, and Space sectors. Evans is a Fellow of the Royal Society of Arts and a Companion of Chartered Management.
